sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds: Battle Born Pond, Davis Creek Pond, Idlewild Ponds, Marilyn's Pond, Paradise Ponds, Rancho San Rafael Pond, Sparks Marina Pond, Verdi Pond, Virginia Lake and Wilson Common. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours parks are open to public use.

sources.title: Sparks Marina Angler and Fish Survey Information — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Fishing Regulations: Season is open year-round, 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset. Limit is 3 game fish. Only vessels without motors and vessels which are powered by electric motors are permitted on Sparks Marina Pond. Vessels are prohibited in areas designated by signs or buoys at the swimming and diving areas. Fishing from a stand-up paddle board is possible in Sparks marina. Fishing is prohibited in swimming and diving areas of Sparks Marina.

sources.title: Sparks Marina — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Rainbow, cutthroat, and brown trout, spotted bass, largemouth bass, smallmouth bass, green sunfish, channel catfish, bullhead catfish, and carp.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Sparks Marina is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit (e.g. for black bass) is stated for Sparks Marina in NDOW's current regulation table or its own angler guide; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds (incl. Sparks Marina)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Sparks Marina.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: NDOW's commission-regulation table lists hours for Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds generically as 'hours parks are open to public use'; NDOW's own Sparks Marina angler guide gives this specific window.

regulations.citation: Sparks Marina Angler and Fish Survey Information (NDOW)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing hours at Sparks Marina: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Season is open year-round, 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older; no water-specific permit exists for Sparks Marina.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older at Sparks Marina; no license is required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Vessels are prohibited in areas designated by signs or buoys at the swimming and diving areas; fishing is also prohibited in those swimming/diving areas.

regulations.citation: Sparks Marina Angler and Fish Survey Information (NDOW)
regulations.humanSummary: Only non-motorized and electric-motor vessels are permitted on Sparks Marina; no gas motors. Vessels and fishing are prohibited in posted swimming/diving areas.
regulations.verbatimText: Only vessels without motors and vessels which are powered by electric motors are permitted on Sparks Marina Pond. Vessels are prohibited in areas designated by signs or buoys at the swimming and diving areas. Fishing from a stand-up paddle board is possible in Sparks marina. Fishing is prohibited in swimming and diving areas of Sparks Marina.
